attack_surface.exposure: Any channel that uses the merged plang serializer to ingest external bytes (post-Stage-2 consolidation widened this surface). `plang.@this.DeserializeAsync` / `Deserialize(string)` route through `WireJsonConverter.Read`, which calls `LiftDataIfShaped` on any JSON object in the `value` slot.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Bytes off the wire are untrusted until signing.verify runs. Wire converter parses the shape first; verify runs against the parsed Data after.
mitigations: STJ MaxDepth=64 caps JSON nesting per ParseValue call
100 MB cap on decompressed bytes in Transport.GZipDecompress
EnsureSupportedValue rejects Data instances on Properties insertion (top-level)
gaps: LiftDataIfShaped resets STJ depth on every nesting level by going through GetRawText()+Deserialize<@this>(string) — see F1
No size limit on compressed input bytes; only on decompressed output (a 100 MB allocation per inbound payload is sustainable but not paged)
EnsureSupportedValue is structural (top-level only) — nested Data inside IDictionary/IEnumerable accepted on insert

attack_surface.exposure: WireJsonConverter.Write fires EnsureSigned on each Data it visits, but only when `data.Context?.Actor != null`. Bare-context Datas serialize unsigned.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Caller responsibility: production paths (Variables.Set / Channels.Register) must wire Actor before any wire crossing; receive-side signing.verify is the trust gate.
mitigations: signing.verify returns NoSignature 400 when RawSignature is null
Documented carveout (handler comment + xmldoc) for test fixtures / .pr authoring / raw IClass calls
gaps: Consumers that dispatch on Data without verifying signature trust wire input — same systemic shape as the standing `callback.run` finding
No 'strict' mode that throws on unsigned egress (would surface mis-wired pipelines before they leak)

attack_surface.exposure: Hash bytes must equal wire bytes (minus the outermost Signature field) so the outer signature transitively binds inner-Data signatures.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Bytes signed must equal bytes verified.
mitigations: crypto/Default.Hash routes through registered application/plang serializer (or ContextLessFallback) so wire == hash
Type-mismatch check on registered serializer returns SerializerMismatch 500 (closed v1 finding F4)
MarkOuterForHash uses Dictionary<@this,int> with ReferenceEqualityComparer + ref-count (closed v1 F2)

attack_surface.exposure: `application/plang` serializer intentionally omits the Sensitive.Strip modifier so settings/sqlite/identity persistence works.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: PLang's settings store is trusted; inter-actor channels carrying plang bytes externally are not.
mitigations: Json/Text serializers DO apply Sensitive.Strip — channels for external publication should pick those
Identity.PrivateKey is the only [Sensitive] field today
gaps: No build-time gate stops a channel from being registered with plang mime for external transport
Stage 2 widens the set of channels that route through plang (callback was already standing finding; transport serializer is now the single home)

attack_surface.exposure: Each property value emitted as `properties.{key}: <primitive|object|array>`. On write, each value is run through STJ which may pick up the WireJsonConverter if the value is itself a Data. On read, ReadPropertyPrimitive parses primitive shapes only — Data shape is not lifted back.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Properties carry metadata; sign-if-missing walks Value-graph Datas only.
mitigations: EnsureSupportedValue rejects Data instances on direct insert
Outer signature canonicalizes the full wire (including Properties), so tampering invalidates the outer signature
gaps: EnsureSupportedValue accepts IDictionary/IEnumerable structurally — a List<Data> would slip through and emit signed Datas on wire that round-trip back as dictionaries (asymmetric)

attack_surface.exposure: Parser owns the `%x!cost%` → (Name="x", Property="cost") split. Malformed shapes (`%x!!cost%`, `%x!a!b%`, `%!x!cost%`) are flagged IsMalformed; write-side fails with InvalidVariableReference 400.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Variable references come from .pr (signed by user) and from action handlers.
mitigations: IsMalformed propagates → variable.set returns typed 400 instead of silently writing to Properties[""] or junk-Name binding (closed v1 F5)
Variable.Resolve never throws — parse errors ride on IsMalformed/Name

attack_surface.exposure: archived.Value is byte[] of gzipped wire JSON. DecompressAsync calls GZipDecompress then serializer.DeserializeAsync.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Compressed bytes are wire-untrusted; decompression must cap expansion.
mitigations: InvalidDataException / JsonException / InvalidOperationException narrowly caught → DecompressError 500
gaps: No cap on compressed input size (caller's responsibility); but decompressed-output cap closes the classic zip-bomb

findings.vector: Recursive Data deserialization through LiftDataIfShaped resets STJ MaxDepth on every level by round-tripping through `JsonElement.GetRawText()` + `JsonSerializer.Deserialize<@this>(string, options)`. The new Deserialize call creates a fresh Utf8JsonReader with depth counter reset to zero. STJ's MaxDepth=64 caps JSON nesting per ParseValue call, but each LiftDataIfShaped recursion is a NEW parse → the depth budget restarts. C# call stack is the only remaining bound.
findings.preconditions: Any path that hands attacker-controlled bytes to plang.Deserialize* — reachable TODAY via the callback wire surface (callback.run deserializes Data from wire before any signature check fires; see standing memory finding). Parsing occurs BEFORE signing.verify runs, so the attacker does not need a verified identity to land the payload. Not contingent on a future external channel.
findings.impact: Pre-auth, unrecoverable host process crash. .NET treats StackOverflow as unrecoverable — no catch unwinds, no error path, just process death. DoS against the deserializing actor/process. Cheap (~11 KB), trivially repeatable, no preconditions beyond wire reachability.
findings.exploit_sketch: Craft application/plang JSON of the shape `{"name":"a","value":{"name":"a","value":{...}}}` nested ~500 times. Each Read sees `value` as StartObject → JsonDocument.ParseValue (one frame, OK within MaxDepth=64) → LiftDataIfShaped detects name+value pair → JsonSerializer.Deserialize<@this>(GetRawText(), options) → new Utf8JsonReader with depth=0 → recursive Read. Roughly 5 stack frames per Data level; default .NET 1 MB stack overflows around 400-600 levels. Payload size ~22 bytes/level × 500 ≈ 11 KB — well under any channel buffer cap. Submit via any inbound application/plang channel (HTTP-receive with Mime=application/plang, callback resume, inter-process pipe, etc.).
findings.proposed_fix: Coder landed option (a) — AsyncLocal recursion-depth counter throwing JsonException past 64 levels. Defense-in-depth, harmless. Worth keeping for the future-MaxDepth-override case.
findings.v2_note: Mutation test (MaxReadDepth=int.MaxValue) showed the 200-level bomb still surfaces PlangDeserializeError — STJ's per-reader MaxDepth=64 is the actual gate, not the new counter. My v1 trace missed that ParseValue inherits the source reader's MaxDepth and can't hand the recursive call a sub-tree deeper than it. So F1 was a false-positive at HIGH; the deserializer was already protected. Coder's fix kept as defense-in-depth.

findings.vector: Stage 2 consolidates application/plang as the single inter-actor transport serializer. The merged class explicitly does NOT chain `Sensitive.Strip` into its outbound options (xmldoc: "PLang's own settings/sqlite store rides on this serializer to persist Identity"). Any channel registered with Mime=application/plang transports [Sensitive] field values in cleartext.
findings.preconditions: An actor registers a non-local channel (HTTP, socket, file, callback) with `Mime=application/plang`. Today only Identity.PrivateKey carries `[Sensitive]`; future additions inherit the gap. Same systemic shape as the standing `pattern_http_module_security.md` and the runtime2-callback v1 finding (callback wire serializers don't apply SensitivePropertyFilter).
findings.impact: PrivateKey (and any future [Sensitive] field) crosses the wire. Caller-of-channel cannot retroactively redact.
findings.exploit_sketch: Register channel: `channel.add name=remote mime=application/plang ...`. Write any Data whose Value graph reaches an Identity object. Wire bytes contain `"privateKey": "...base64..."`. Receive side reads it. Out-of-band capture of wire bytes also works.
findings.proposed_fix: Two-track. (a) Document the rule at channel-registration time: if Mime=application/plang AND channel is external (not in-process settings/sqlite), wrap outbound through a Sensitive.Strip-augmented options layer. (b) Architectural cleaner: split into `application/plang-persisted` (no Strip, used by settings/sqlite only) vs `application/plang` (with Strip, default for inter-actor transport). Lower priority while only Identity.PrivateKey is in scope, but the architectural fix should land before any other [Sensitive] field ships.

findings.vector: `Properties.EnsureSupportedValue` is a top-level structural gate: scalars exhaustively checked, but `IDictionary` / `IEnumerable` accepted without recursing into elements. A `List<Data>` or `Dictionary<string, Data>` slips through and is stored as-is. On wire write each contained Data goes through WireJsonConverter (sign-if-missing fires — inner attestations are emitted). On wire read, ReadPropertyPrimitive parses primitive shapes only — never invokes LiftDataIfShaped — so the inner Data round-trips as a Dictionary. The signed inner attestation IS in the bytes but is unreachable post-deserialize.
findings.preconditions: A producer (test fixture, action handler, .pr author) calls `data.Properties[key] = someListContainingData`. Accepted as tradeoff in codeanalyzer v1 F9 — the rationale being `List<LlmMessage>` shape today.
findings.impact: Asymmetric round-trip: producer expects Data attestations in Properties (they aren't reachable post-deserialize), or attestations the consumer believed unreachable are present in the bytes (signed but invisible). Confused-API risk rather than direct exploit. Outer signature still binds the wire bytes so tampering is detected.
findings.exploit_sketch: Producer: `data.Properties["messages"] = new List<Data> { childData };`. Wire emit signs childData (sign-if-missing). Wire read: properties.messages comes back as `List<object?>` of dictionaries — childData.Signature is in the bytes but unreachable through `data.Properties.Get<Data>("messages")`. Consumer that trusts presence-in-Properties as a security signal would be confused.
findings.proposed_fix: Long-term: tighten EnsureSupportedValue to recurse (or fail loudly) on container leaves. Short-term: keep coder's v1 F9 deferral but document the asymmetry in Properties.xmldoc with an explicit "Data in Properties is opaque post-deserialize" note. Acceptable to defer if producers are audited at upstream call sites.

findings.vector: Sign-if-missing in WireJsonConverter.Write silently skips EnsureSigned when `data.Context?.Actor == null`. Same shape as the standing finding about `callback.run` skipping verify when RawSignature==null: a permissive branch on the shared dispatch path. Receive-side signing.verify catches the absence (returns NoSignature 400), so the gate holds today.
findings.preconditions: An attacker would need to (a) cause Data with no Actor to reach a wire-egress AND (b) get a consumer to trust the wire bytes without calling signing.verify. (a) requires misconfigured production code (the xmldoc claims production always wires Actor); (b) requires a consumer pattern that skips verification.
findings.impact: Defense-in-depth gap. Today consumer-side verify is the gate; the wire converter's permissive skip is a 'paper, not security' defense rather than primary trust. Risk turns Medium if any consumer pattern emerges that gates trust on Signature-presence without calling signing.verify.
findings.exploit_sketch: Not directly exploitable today — would require pairing with a verify-skipping consumer. Standing memory: `callback.run` skips verify when RawSignature==null is the matching consumer-side gap; flipping both together would breach the model.
findings.proposed_fix: Add a `RequireSignature=true` mode (channel-level or serializer-options-level) where the wire converter throws `InvalidOperationException` rather than silently skipping. Wire it default-on for non-in-process channels. The current skip stays for the documented test/fixture paths.

summary: v2 update: F1 retracted from HIGH to Info after mutation testing. Setting MaxReadDepth=int.MaxValue did not change the 200-level depth-bomb outcome — STJ's per-reader MaxDepth=64 was already the gate. My v1 reasoning treated each LiftDataIfShaped → Deserialize<@this>(string) recursion as depth-reset → unbounded; but ParseValue inherits the source reader's MaxDepth and can't hand the recursive call a sub-tree deeper than it. Coder's AsyncLocal counter is kept as defense-in-depth (covers the future case where a caller raises options.MaxDepth). Architecture-of-trust layer remains clean: canonicalization correct, outer signature binds inner Datas, `!` parser fails closed, zip-bomb defense (100 MB) in place. F2/F3/F4 unchanged. Verdict: PASS.
